candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line, but it appears below gross profit and alongside operating expense buckets rather than within or alongside the selected COGS row 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. No COGS-specific D&A label is shown.
Component of the total D&A line, but not identified as COGS-related. Using it for COGS would be unsupported and risks misclassification.
Specific depreciation component under the broader D&A section, with no indication it belongs to COGS.
Component of the total D&A line, but not labeled as COGS-related and therefore should not be separately added to COGS.

variables.notes: The selected COGS bucket is the explicit total 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. The visible D&A rows are presented later in the operating section as separate operating-expense lines, not as COGS-specific rows. There is no explicit D&A row tied to cost of revenues, so conservatively do not add any D&A separately to COGS.

candidates.why: This is the explicit summary D&A line above EBIT and separate from the selected XSGA component rows ('Staff costs', 'Research and development costs are capitalized', 'Salaries Activated During Development'). Because it sits as a separate same-level operating line, it should be added separately rather than assumed embedded in those selected rows.
Appears to be a component of the total 'Depreciation and amortization expense'. Do not choose it when the summary row exists, to avoid double counting.
Appears to be a component of the total D&A line. Exclude in favor of the summary row.
Appears to be a component of the total D&A line. Exclude in favor of the summary row.

variables.notes: Use the summary D&A row only. It is a separate operating-expense line above EBIT and not a sub-component of the selected XSGA rows. No SG&A-specific D&A split is provided, so the explicit total operating D&A line is the best separate add-back candidate for XSGA_DA.
